participants.name: 화학공학
participants.professional_persona: 화학공학 박사학위를 받고 현재 부산대학교의 BK21 교육연구단에서 박사후연구원으로 재직 중입니다. 유기합성과 촉매반응을 전공하며 특히 친환경 공정 개발에 집중하고 있습니다.
participants.skills_and_expertise: 유기합성 및 촉매 설계 분야에서 3년간의 연구 경험을 보유하고 있으며, 고압 반응기와 NMR, GC-MS 분석에 능숙합니다.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: 안정적인 연구 환경 속에서 차세대 친환경 촉매를 개발하여 국내 정밀화학 산업 경쟁력에 기여하고, 장기적으로는 대학 교수로 자리잡고 싶습니다.

participants.name: 한양대학교 (jduobt)
participants.professional_persona: 한양대학교 전자공학부 조교수로서 미래인재양성사업 교육연구단의 연구교수로 활동 중입니다. 3년 전 미국에서 박사 후 연구를 마치고 귀국했습니다.
participants.skills_and_expertise: 반도체 소자 및 집적회로 설계, 연구 성과 분석 및 컨설팅 보고서 작성 경험.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: 교육연구단의 국제화 역량을 높이고, 참여 대학원생들의 우수 논문 게재를 지원하여 연구단 평가 지표를 개선하는 것이 목표입니다.

participants.name: 경상북도
participants.professional_persona: 경상북도 소재 지방국립대학교에서 식물분자생리학을 연구하는 박사수료생입니다. 농업 생명공학 전공으로, BK21 사업단을 통해 작물 스트레스 내성 유전자 기능 연구를 수행하며 월 100만 원의 장학금을 지원받고 있습니다.
participants.skills_and_expertise: 유전자 클로닝, CRISPR 유전자 편집, 형질전환체 제작 및 표현형 분석에 숙련되어 있습니다. 현장 실험과 실험실 작업을 병행하는 데 강점이 있습니다.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: 기후 변화에 대응하는 고부가가치 작물 개발에 이바지하고자 농촌진흥청 또는 종자 기업 연구소에 취업하는 것을 희망합니다. 지역 농업 발전에 기여하는 것도 중요하게 생각합니다.

participants.name: 부산
participants.entity_name: Education Research Group Participants and Graduate Innovation Practitioners | KG-Gen-j6xuhc43
participants.representative_type: Education Research Group Participants and Graduate Innovation Practitioners | KG-Gen-j6xuhc43
participants.professional_persona: 부산 지역 사립대학에서 화학공학 석사 과정에 재학 중이며, BK21 혁신인재양성사업단에 소속되어 에너지 소재 연구를 진행하고 있습니다. 월 70만 원의 장학금으로 생활하며, 대학원 혁신을 위한 온라인 간담회에 적극 참여하고 있습니다.
participants.skills_and_expertise: 전기화학 분석, 배터리 소재 합성 및 특성 평가에 전문성을 갖추었으며, 실험 데이터 분석에 파이썬을 활용할 줄 압니다.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: 석사 졸업 후 국책 연구소에 취업하여 차세대 이차전지 기술 개발에 기여하고, 장기적으로는 에너지 분야 전문가로 성장하고자 합니다.

participants.name: 서울대학교
participants.professional_persona: 서울대학교 사회학과에서 박사학위를 받고 현재 동 대학 BK21 교육연구단에서 신진연구자로 활동 중입니다. 디지털 전환과 사회적 불평등에 관한 연구를 수행하고 있습니다.
participants.skills_and_expertise: 대규모 설문조사 설계 및 통계 분석(SPSS, Stata)에 능숙하며, 질적 연구 방법론과 혼합연구법에도 강점이 있습니다.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: BK21 지원을 통해 데이터 기반 사회정책 연구를 심화하여, 디지털 격차 해소에 기여하는 연구자로 성장하고 싶습니다.

initial_posts.narrative: As a BK21-supported doctoral candidate at a regional national university, I see the total funding allocated to Hanyang University's BK21 program as the sum of education research group project budgets, which is 355,151 million KRW per the policy context. This is the amount that directly supports graduate scholarships and postdoc salaries. For graduate scholarship slots, the policy states about 12,600 students per year in future talent training and 6,400 in innovative talent training, totaling about 19,000 slots nationally. Hanyang's share would be proportional to its number of education research groups. For postdoc positions, the minimum salary is 3 million KRW per month, and the budget for early-career researchers is part of the project budget. Given typical group sizes and budget constraints, I estimate around 1,200 postdoc positions across all BK21 groups at Hanyang, though this is a rough estimate based on my experience in a smaller group.

initial_posts.narrative: As a BK21-supported doctoral candidate at a regional national university in Gyeongsangbuk-do, I see the outputs phase as directly translating the funded slots into research activity. With 19,000 graduate scholarship slots and 1,200 postdoc positions funded, the actual number of supported individuals matches these slots because the program fully funds them. Based on my own experience and observing peers in my lab and other groups, a typical graduate student produces about 2-3 publications per year (including journal articles and conference papers), while postdocs produce about 4-5. Given that many students are in early stages and not all are equally productive, I conservatively estimate an average of 3 publications per supported participant (graduate + postdoc) per year. With 20,200 total participants, that yields about 60,600 publications annually, but since the simulation target is for 2022 and some participants may be less active, I round to 57,000. For international conference presentations, I estimate about 20% of participants present at an international conference each year, based on my lab's experience where about 1 in 5 students present abroad annually. That gives about 3,800 presentations. These estimates are grounded in my personal experience in a regional university, where productivity is somewhat lower than top-tier institutions but still substantial due to BK21 support.
evidence: The 19,000 graduate scholarship slots and 1,200 postdoc positions are fully funded, so the number of supported individuals equals these slot counts.
In my lab and similar regional university labs, graduate students typically produce 2-3 publications per year, and postdocs produce 4-5, leading to an average of about 3 per participant.
International conference participation is limited by funding and travel opportunities; in my experience, about 20% of BK21 participants present at an international conference each year.
The policy context emphasizes research output and internationalization, but regional universities like mine have fewer resources for international travel, so the rate is moderate.

initial_posts.narrative: As a postdoc at Pusan National University in a BK21 education research group, I see the outcomes phase as the result of the inputs, activities, and outputs phases. The total funding of 355,151 million KRW and disbursement of 408,080 million KRW (including carryover) have supported 19,000 graduate students and 1,200 postdocs, who produced 57,000 publications and 3,800 international conference presentations. These outputs directly contribute to the international collaboration rate, as conference presentations often lead to joint projects and co-authored papers. Based on my experience, about 20% of participants are involved in international collaborations, yielding 3,800 collaborations. For employment, the program's focus on research output and networking, combined with the consulting and evaluation feedback loops, helps graduates secure jobs. I estimate a graduate employment rate of 78.9% (about 15,000 employed out of 19,000 graduates), slightly below the national target of 86.7% due to the declining trend noted in the policy context. The full-time faculty lecture ratio is high at 85%, as the program incentivizes hiring full-time faculty for better mentoring and educational quality, which I observe in my group where most lectures are by full-time professors.
evidence: The policy context states that the employment rate target is set to increase by 0.3% per year from the 2010-2012 average of 86.7%, but considering the declining trend, I estimate a realistic 78.9% for 2022 based on my observation of job market conditions for chemistry graduates.

International collaboration rate is estimated at 20% based on my lab's experience where about 1 in 5 researchers participate in joint projects or exchanges, and the 3,800 international conference presentations from the outputs phase support this.

Full-time faculty lecture ratio is high because the program's evaluation criteria reward full-time faculty involvement, and in my group, over 85% of graduate lectures are taught by full-time professors, which improves mentoring quality.

The consulting and evaluation feedback loops from the activities phase (280 consulting sessions, 3 evaluation panels) help groups improve their performance, leading to better employment outcomes and collaboration rates.

initial_posts.narrative: As a BK21-supported doctoral candidate at a regional national university in Gyeongsangbuk-do, I see the Impact phase as the long-term result of sustained investment in graduate talent development over a decade. The prior phases show that 19,000 graduate students and 1,200 postdocs were supported, producing 57,000 publications and 3,800 international conference presentations in 2022. These outputs, combined with the dissemination of outstanding group achievements and operational know-how through the BK21 webzine and forums, gradually improve the national S&T ecosystem. Over a decade, the program's focus on research integrity, collaboration, and interdisciplinarity—fostered by mandatory online education and consulting—shifts research culture. Alumni progress in their careers, becoming faculty or industry leaders who promote these values. However, as a regional university student, I see that top-tier institutions (Seoul, KAIST) dominate high-impact research, so South Korea's global rank improves modestly from around 10th to 8th. High-impact publications per 1000 researchers increase from about 10 to 12.5, driven by sustained funding and cultural change, but regional disparities limit faster progress. I note that other participants may estimate higher ranks based on top-university experiences, but my regional perspective grounds me in realistic, slower improvements.
evidence: Sustained BK21 funding over a decade supports a large pool of trained researchers, but national competitiveness rank improves slowly due to global competition and regional disparities; I estimate a rank of 8 based on Korea's current position around 10th and gradual improvement.

High-impact publications per 1000 researchers increase from about 10 to 12.5, as the program's emphasis on research integrity and collaboration boosts quality, but regional universities like mine produce fewer top-cited papers than elite institutions.

The dissemination of outstanding group achievements through the BK21 webzine and forums spreads best practices, but adoption at regional universities is slower, limiting the overall impact on national metrics.
Alumni from the program who become faculty or industry leaders promote a healthier research culture, but this effect takes years to materialize, so the 10-year impact is moderate.
